messages.content:
You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

A soft rounded-square badge in warm cream sits behind everything as the base shape, giving a friendly frame like a piece of paper; a thick wobbly crayon-style circle outline in coral traces just inside the badge edge, drawn slightly irregular as if hand-drawn by a child; behind the main icon, a small cluster of five short crayon-stroke rays in yellow and teal radiate from the top-left corner suggesting a sun and creative spark; centered in the badge, a stylized paintbrush tilted diagonally with a chunky wooden handle in mustard yellow and a soft teal ferrule; from the brush tip flows a bouncy ribbon-like swirl of paint in coral, teal, and yellow that curls into a small circular blob at the bottom right, like a dollop of paint pooling; a tiny five-point sparkle in white sits near the top right of the swirl to add whimsy and shine; the whole composition is centered and balanced with slightly imperfect, rounded, hand-crafted linework to feel approachable and fun for children.

stop-color="#d1922a"/></linearGradient></defs><rect x="20" y="20" width="216" height="216" rx="38" fill="#f7ecd8"/><g stroke="#e8925f" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"><path d="M40 65 Q35 30 65 40"/><path d="M35 90 Q28 60 60 63"/></g><g stroke="#f0c542" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"><path d="M50 45 Q42 20 68 25"/><path d="M70 35 Q65 12 90 20"/></g><g 168 216 133 C213 100 198 68 172 48 C160 39 145 32 128 30 Z" fill="none" stroke="#e8724c" stroke-width="11" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><g><rect x="0" y="0" width="22" height="70" rx="9" fill="url(#handle)" transform="translate(108,58) rotate(38)"/><rect x="-3" y="58" width="28" height="22" rx="7" fill="#5fbdb2" transform="translate(108,58) rotate(38)"/><path d="M140 118 Q150 128 146 140 Q142 152 130 150" fill="none" stroke="#3fa89d" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/></g><path d="M140 118 C150 132 175 128 182 148 C189 168 172 185 152 182 C165 175 168 160 155 150 C148 158 150 172 162 178 C150 190 132 186 128 170 C124 156 136 148 148 152" fill="none" stroke="#e8724c" stroke-width="11" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><path d="M148 152 C158 158 168 170 160 182 C152 194 134 192 128 178" fill="none" stroke="#4fb3a9" stroke-width="9" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M132 176 C140 168 152 168 158 178" fill="none" stroke="#f0c542" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round"/><circle cx="162" cy="188" r="17" fill="#e8724c"/><circle cx="162" cy="188" r="17" fill="none" stroke="#c85a3a" stroke-width="3"/><path d="M182 108 L186 118 L196 122 L186 126 L182 136 L178 126 L168 122 L178 118 Z" fill="#ffffff"/></svg>

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

A soft circular badge in pale gray-blue (#E8ECEF) sits centered as the base, giving the mark a stable, coin-like foundation; behind the main figures, a simplified rising sun with three clean rays fans upward in warm orange (#F2994A) from the lower portion of the circle, symbolizing hope after crisis; in front of the sun, a simplified roofline made of two bold triangular strokes in deep blue (#1F4E79) forms a small house shape, representing shelter and home; cradling the house from below, a single open hand rendered in the same deep blue as a smooth, minimal silhouette curves upward and outward, its fingers gently spread to suggest care, support, and rescue; a thin white outline separates the hand and house from the sun and background circle to keep the composition crisp and legible at small sizes; the overall silhouette is symmetric and centered, designed to read clearly as an icon even without accompanying text.

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a rounded amber bottle silhouette with a narrow neck and gentle shoulders, filled solid with deep forest green to root the mark in craft brewing tradition; inside the bottle, layer a concentric swirl pattern made of soft organic rings in olive-green and mustard-orange, mimicking a SCOBY culture floating in liquid; scatter three to five small circular bubbles of pale cream rising from the base toward the neck to suggest carbonation and fermentation; wrap a thin curved line across the widest part of the bottle like a label band, left unlettered but subtly textured with tiny dashes to imply craft paper; finish with a small sprig of two leaves sprouting from the bottle cap area in muted sage green, symbolizing natural, botanical ingredients; keep all shapes rounded and hand-drawn in feel, with slightly uneven, organic edges rather than perfect geometry to reinforce the artisanal, small-batch character.

- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a rounded circular badge in deep charcoal (#2D2A26) as a solid backing plate to give the mark weight and contrast against the cream background; inside it, draw a simplified smoothie cup shape in soft off-white, wider at the top and tapering to a rounded base, with a thick straight straw poking out at an angle from the top left in coral (#FF6F59); fill the upper portion of the cup with a swirling spiral of color bands in mango yellow (#FFC145), coral (#FF6F59), and leafy green (#4CAF7D) to suggest whirling blended fruit; place a few small rounded dots of the same three colors scattered just above the cup rim to read as flying fruit droplets or bubbles; add a thin white rim highlight along the left edge of the cup for a glossy, fresh look; keep all shapes simple, bold, and geometric with smooth rounded corners so the mark reads clearly at small sizes.

- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Centered on a clean white background, a large soft-edged circle in warm orange forms the base like a cross-section of citrus fruit; inside it, thin curved white lines radiate from the center like orange segments to suggest a slice; layered on top, a bright yellow-green droplet shape rises from the bottom, its rounded body overlapping the fruit circle to imply a smoothie splash; a small white highlight oval sits on the upper left of the droplet to give it a glossy, liquid feel; two tiny green leaf shapes sprout from the top of the droplet, curving outward to add a fresh, natural touch; the entire icon is drawn with smooth, friendly rounded edges and bold flat colors to feel playful, healthy, and energetic.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><rect x="-9999" y="-9999" width="19998" height="19998" fill="#ffffff"/><defs><radialGradient id="orangeGrad" cx="40%" cy="35%" r="70%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffb15e"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#f7902e"/></radialGradient><linearGradient id="dropGrad" x1="20%" y1="0%" x2="80%" y2="100%"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#d4ea3a"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#a8d321"/></linearGradient></defs><circle cx="128" cy="118" r="78" fill="url(#orangeGrad)"/><g stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.9"><path d="M128 118 L128 46"/><path d="M128 118 L184 66"/><path d="M128 118 L196 108"/><path d="M128 118 L188 168"/><path d="M128 118 L138 190"/><path d="M128 118 L78 182"/><path d="M128 118 L64 128"/><path d="M128 118 L72 68"/></g><circle cx="128" cy="118" r="17" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.85"/><path d="M108 118 C 60 150, 50 205, 90 232 C 120 252, 160 248, 178 220 C 196 192, 182 150, 148 118 C 138 108, 118 108, 108 118 Z" fill="url(#dropGrad)"/><path d="M118 122 C 100 108, 92 82, 104 60 C 116 82, 118 108, 128 122 Z" fill="#5aa72a"/><path d="M132 120 C 138 96, 160 78, 184 78 C 174 100, 156 114, 140 124 Z" fill="#6fbf2c"/><ellipse cx="96" cy="165" rx="18" ry="11" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.8" transform="rotate(-25 96 165)"/></svg>

- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a soft circular backdrop in pale sky blue (#8ECAE6) representing calm and openness; within it place a simplified human head-in-profile silhouette facing right, rendered in deep slate blue (#2A4759) with a smooth, gentle outline to feel approachable rather than clinical; inside the head, instead of a solid fill, show a rising sun made of concentric warm-orange (#F4A261) arcs peeking up from the base of the skull area to symbolize hope and new beginnings; where the forehead meets the top of the head, morph the silhouette into a few open petal shapes in medium blue (#5B8FB9) that read as a small blossoming flower, symbolizing growth and self-care; beneath the head silhouette, add a simple open cupped-hand arc in the same slate blue, subtly cradling the head shape to convey support and community; keep all linework clean, rounded, and minimal with generous negative space so the mark reads clearly at small sizes.'

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.

- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Centered on a soft cream background, a large single hexagon outlined in deep amber forms the backbone of the mark, evoking a honeycomb cell; behind it, faint smaller hexagons tile outward at low opacity to suggest a broader honeycomb pattern without cluttering the focal shape; inside the main hexagon, a smooth golden honey drop shape sits slightly bottom-weighted, rendered in a rich gradient from warm amber at the top to deeper burnt-orange at the base, with a small rounded highlight of pale cream to give it a glossy, dripping look; a thin trail of honey drips from the bottom tip of the drop, ending in one small separate droplet beneath the hexagon for added craft detail; a simple, friendly bee silhouette in dark brown with two soft translucent wings rests atop the honey drop, wings slightly overlapping the drop's highlight to tie the elements together; the overall composition is symmetrical and iconic, balanced within the circular negative space of the hexagon, suitable for a premium specialty honey brand mark.

Center a soft circular badge in dark charcoal (#2B2B2B) as a subtle backdrop disc for contrast; on top, draw a simplified white chef's toque (puffy rounded top with a fitted band) filling the upper two-thirds of the badge; inside the puffy top of the hat, nest a small warm flame shape in gradient orange-to-red (#F4A83B to #D64541) to suggest passion and fire in cooking; beneath the hat's band, cross two slender fork silhouettes in white, tines pointing outward like a laurel to imply personalized, hands-on culinary craft; add a thin white ring outline just inside the badge edge to frame the composition and give it an emblem, seal-like finish; keep all shapes flat, bold, and geometrically simplified for clean scalability at small sizes.

A dark forest-green hexagon badge with a slightly rounded outline sits centered as the base emblem shape, edged with a thin golden border for a rustic stamped look; inside, a lighter green concentric hexagon ring creates depth like a woodcut frame; behind the main icon, faint radiating lines suggest sunbeams breaking through trees; centered in the badge, a tall stylized pine tree silhouette in deep forest green forms the backdrop, its trunk widening at the base like a mug handle; overlapping the tree at the bottom third, a simplified beer mug silhouette in cream color holds a foamy golden-tan top that blends into the tree's lower branches, merging the two forms into one icon; small hop-leaf shapes in golden mustard flank the mug on either side, echoing pinecones or hops; along the inner curve of the hexagon frame, a subtle rope or wood-grain texture line in muted brown adds a handcrafted, rustic border detail.'

Centered on a dark charcoal-brown circular badge background, a weathered stone archway forms the outer frame suggesting ancient ruins; inside the arch, a classical fluted column stands vertically at the center, its shaft cracked and slightly eroded like an archaeological relic; the column's vertical flutes transition seamlessly at the top into three curved sound-wave arcs radiating outward like a podcast signal icon, rendered in warm gold; a small sun disk or ankh-like symbol sits atop the column capital, hinting at Egyptian and Mesopotamian motifs; a thin decorative border of simple geometric meander (Greek key) pattern traces the inner edge of the circular badge; all linework uses a warm gold and sandy beige palette against the deep brown background to evoke aged papyrus and desert dusk.

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a thin perfect circle outline in soft cream as a quiet backdrop halo, giving the mark a boutique, contained feel; inside it, place a sleek abstract silhouette combining an open pair of scissors and a flowing curl of hair rendered as one continuous black ribbon line with rounded, tapering strokes; let the scissor blades curve outward on the upper right like elegant wings while the handles loop smoothly into a wavy strand of hair sweeping to the lower left, implying motion and softness; add one delicate gold accent dot at the scissor pivot point to serve as a focal jewel and a touch of chic luxury; keep all linework uniform-width and minimal with generous negative space so the icon reads cleanly at small sizes; finish with a subtle thin gold ring just inside the cream circle's edge to frame the composition like a refined emblem.

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a soft rounded circle badge in pale sky blue as the background shape, giving the logo a friendly coin-like frame. Behind the top of the circle, place a simple bright yellow sun with a few short rounded rays peeking over the rim to suggest a cheerful outdoor pool day. Across the lower half of the circle, layer two overlapping wave bands in teal and deeper blue, drawn as smooth rounded curves like gentle ripples of a pool. Swimming just above the waves, place a small chubby cartoon fish in coral red with a round white belly, a single dot eye, and a curved smile, tilted slightly as if mid-swim. Add a few small white bubble circles of varying size trailing behind the fish to reinforce motion through water. Finish with a slightly thicker white outline around the entire circular badge to give it a clean, sticker-like finish suitable for a kids' brand.

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a soft rounded circle in warm off-white (#FFF8F0) as the base plate background, giving the mark a friendly badge shape; behind it place a subtle teal (#2EC4B6) drop shadow offset slightly down-right for depth; inside the circle draw a stylized plate as a simple thin dark ring (#1B1B1E); atop the plate place a bold orange (#FF6B35) speed-swoosh shape curving from lower-left to upper-right like a comet trail, symbolizing fast delivery; where the swoosh tapers to its point, morph it into a simple fork silhouette with two rounded tines, so the fork appears to be zooming across the plate; add a small teal circular dot trailing behind the swoosh's tail to reinforce motion, like a motion-blur speed dot; keep all shapes geometric, rounded, and flat with no gradients so the logo stays crisp and iconic at small app-icon sizes.

Center a large soft-edged circle in deep navy as the background badge, giving weight and a tailoring-guild feel; inside it, place a slender golden needle angled diagonally from lower-left to upper-right, its eye clearly rendered as a small oval near the top; thread a curving gold line through the needle's eye that loops downward and forms a graceful figure-eight stitch pattern beneath it, suggesting a spool of thread unwound; cross a pair of open tailor's scissors in burgundy behind the needle at a slight angle, handles visible at the bottom, blades peeking above the thread loop; add a thin cream-colored ring just inside the navy circle's edge to frame the composition like a button or spool rim; finish with a tiny measuring-tape tick pattern along the inner ring's lower arc in muted gold to reinforce the tailoring craft.”}}

A soft circular backdrop in pale off-white fills the canvas edge to edge, giving the mark room to breathe; centered within it, a gently curved vertical spine made of 6-7 rounded vertebra segments stacked and slightly offset to suggest an S-curve of natural alignment, rendered in deep teal-blue; a thin flowing line threads through the vertebrae like a nerve or energy path, colored in a lighter teal to suggest connectivity and wellness; an open, cupped hand shape wraps gently around the lower half of the spine from behind, rendered in warm orange, symbolizing care and adjustment; a small radiant burst of three short curved lines sits above the topmost vertebra, colored in the same warm orange, representing relief and renewed energy; the entire composition is balanced symmetrically on a vertical axis, kept simple and iconic enough to scale down for a favicon.

Center a dark navy circular backdrop that gives the mark depth and contrast against the background; inset within it, place a bold octagonal shield outline rendered in a bright violet-to-cyan gradient, evoking a controller badge or achievement icon; behind the shield, add a subtle radiating glow in cyan to suggest a screen or power-up energy; inside the octagon, place a crisp white triangular play-button arrow angled slightly forward, symbolizing gameplay and forward momentum; overlay two thin geometric notches at the top corners of the octagon styled like joystick nubs or controller buttons in cyan, adding a playful mechanical detail; finish with a thin outer ring stroke in cyan encircling the whole badge to unify the shape and give it a polished, emblematic studio-logo feel.

You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.

A soft circular badge sits at the back as a pale sage-green disc, grounding the mark with a sense of wholeness and the earth. Behind the upper half of the circle, a simplified radiant sun made of three short thick rays peeks up in warm deep-green tone, symbolizing hope and growth. In the center, a large stylized leaf shape rises like a flame or sapling, its single bold vein curving upward, rendered in a rich forest-green gradient from dark at the base to bright at the tip. Beneath the leaf, two simplified cupped hand forms curve inward from left and right like a shallow bowl, rendered in a deeper teal-green, symbolizing protection, community, and stewardship cradling the leaf/sun above them. A thin circular outline traces just inside the badge edge, unifying all elements into one cohesive emblem suitable for small-scale reproduction.
